title: ageratina hartii
intro: Ageratina hartii, also known as Mexican Tea or Tree Daphne, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of diterpenoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ageratina hartii include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and alleviating menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. Ageratina hartii can be prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water or as a supplement in capsule or tincture form, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
